ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken-based composite dish — taco with chicken filling
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for chicken bits tacos; A0CKP (composite poultry dish) used as closest defensible anchor. Exact code not available in FoodEx2 v2 hierarchy for this specific preparation.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for chicken bits tacos as a composite dish; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.description: 3 pieces of tacos with fillings and toppings
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 3 tacos with chicken bits, toppings, and condiments, weighing approximately 300 g.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Bits Tacos — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate), sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, and cholesterol sourced directly from HPB Foodo at confidence 0.95. Sugar, saturated fat, trans fat, and vitamin C were reported in the input at lower confidence (0.7) from a delivery platform source; these have been retained as the best available values but flagged as lower-confidence estimates pending verification against an approved secondary source such as USDA FoodData Central.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Mixed Chicken and Tortilla Dish — analogous estimate based on corn tortilla taco (GI ~52) and chicken composite dish components
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Chicken Bits Tacos. GI estimated at 50 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for corn tortilla taco (GI ~52, medium) and the moderating effect of protein (6.69 g/100g) and dietary fibre (3.0 g/100g) from chicken and vegetable fillings, which lower the effective glycaemic response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (50 × 45.3) / 100 = 22.7, where carbohydrate per 300 g serving = 15.1 × 3 = 45.3 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 363 mg/100g (1089 mg per 300 g serving) approaches the daily limit of 2000 mg; partially offset by potassium at 229 mg/100g (687 mg per serving).
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one or two tacos, request low-sodium seasoning, and avoid adding extra cheese or sour cream, which contribute additional sodium and saturated fat.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate at 15.1 g/100g with estimated GI of 50 (low) yields a glycemic load of 22.7 per 300 g serving, which is above the low-GL threshold of 10; dietary fibre at 3.0 g/100g and protein at 6.69 g/100g moderate the glycaemic response.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should choose corn tortillas over flour tortillas for a lower GI, limit portion to one or two tacos, and pair with non-starchy vegetables to reduce the overall glycemic load of the meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 4.0 g/100g (12.0 g per 300 g serving) and trans fat at 0.17 g/100g (0.51 g per serving) are clinically relevant for cardiovascular risk; cholesterol at 19 mg/100g is moderate.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish to occasional consumption, choose grilled rather than fried chicken where possible, and avoid adding cheese or sour cream to keep saturated fat and trans fat intake within recommended daily limits.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat at 4.0 g/100g is below the 5 g/100g RA threshold; vitamin C at 5.0 mg/100g provides antioxidant support; nightshade ingredients (tomato, chilli) may aggravate symptoms in sensitive individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should monitor for nightshade sensitivity (tomato, chilli, pepper) and limit cheese and sour cream to keep saturated fat below 5 g/100g per 100 g consumed.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Bits Tacos is a Western-style composite dish popular in Singapore, consisting of crispy fried chicken pieces served in taco shells (corn or flour tortillas) with toppings such as shredded lettuce, diced tomato, cheese, sour cream, and salsa. The dish delivers a satisfying combination of crunchy shell, juicy seasoned chicken, and fresh vegetable toppings. Per 100 g, it provides 170 kcal, 6.69 g protein, 9.17 g fat (of which 4.0 g is saturated), 15.1 g carbohydrate, 3.0 g dietary fibre, and 363 mg sodium. The estimated glycemic index is 50 (low), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 22.7 per standard 300 g serving of three tacos. Clinically, the moderate sodium content (363 mg/100g) warrants caution for individuals managing hypertension, while the low GI and moderate carbohydrate load require portion control for those with Type 2 Diabetes. The saturated fat and trans fat content are relevant for cardiovascular disease management. The dish is typically halal-certified in Singapore food service contexts. It is suitable as a lunch or dinner option and is commonly available through quick-service restaurants and food delivery platforms in Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in chicken bits tacos?
Is chicken taco high in sodium?
What is the GI of chicken tacos?
Are chicken tacos halal in Singapore?
How much protein in chicken tacos?

confidence_reason: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) sourced directly from HPB Foodo at confidence 0.95, which is an approved primary source for Singapore dishes. Sugar, saturated fat, trans fat, and vitamin C were reported in the input at lower confidence (0.7) from a delivery platform source; these have been retained as the best available values but flagged as lower-confidence estimates pending verification against an approved secondary source such as USDA FoodData Central.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite chicken taco dish is not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for this dish and are not available from an approved secondary source for this specific composite preparation as of 2026-04-16. Sugar (1.67 g/100g), saturated_fat_g (4.0 g/100g), trans_fat_g (0.17 g/100g), and vitamin_c_mg (5.0 mg/100g) were provided in the input at confidence 0.7 from a delivery platform source; these are retained as best available estimates pending verification against an approved source. GI of 50 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database corn tortilla taco entry (GI ~52), moderated by protein and fibre content. Halal status is assumed based on typical Singapore food service context but should be verified against the specific vendor's halal certification. The dish contains nightshade ingredients (tomato, chilli, pepper) which may be relevant for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis.
